boundary: Public repo artifacts only. These frames test structure and evidence-ref presence, not semantic truth.

frame.question: How should ToneSoul frame an AI cognitive engine without overclaiming hidden understanding?

temporal_context.text: The externalized cognition architecture is a canonical architecture anchor dated 2026-03-22/2026-03-23.

spatial_context.text: The frame is scoped to public repository architecture documents.

actors.text: Future AI agents and maintainers are the stated audience of the architecture anchor.

known_facts.text: The architecture direction prefers external, inspectable modules over opaque model-internal dependencies.
The claim-to-evidence matrix warns against promoting philosophical claims into mechanism claims.

hypotheses.text: A CognitiveFrame contract can reduce hidden overclaim before answer synthesis.

unknowns.text: Whether the first schema lanes are sufficient for real project work is not yet measured.

constraints.text: Private memory and governance-critical control points must remain external and deletable.

next_probes.text: Validate the frame against additional public artifacts before wiring it into runtime.

frame.question: How should a cognitive frame preserve the responsibility-runtime enforcement boundary?

temporal_context.text: The responsibility-native runtime architecture memo is dated 2026-06-27.

spatial_context.text: The frame is scoped to the public responsibility-runtime design and gate-eval artifacts.

actors.text: The model proposes, the authorization and enforcer layers decide and execute, and trace records the result.

known_facts.text: The runtime architecture says model weights can learn responsibility grammar but must not be the enforcement path.
The gate eval is directional and explicitly not a non-bypassability proof.

hypotheses.text: CognitiveFrame should produce intent-ready context, not bypass authorization.

unknowns.text: Whether future frame-to-intent composition needs a policy decision is unresolved.

constraints.text: Auditee must not possess the enforcement path.

next_probes.text: Add a separate trace-binding cut before using frames to drive runtime actions.

frame.question: How should a cognitive frame describe memory acknowledgements without inventing persistence?

temporal_context.text: The memory output surface eval is dated 2026-06-28.

spatial_context.text: The frame is scoped to public memory-output and memory-claim eval reports.

actors.text: The output surface may acknowledge memory writes only when enforcer result and trace both support it.

known_facts.text: The memory output surface eval reports four deterministic scenarios and zero failures.
Consent language does not replace trace support for memory claims.

hypotheses.text: CognitiveFrame should treat memory claims as trace-dependent output facts, not as model self-report.

unknowns.text: Whether future frame synthesis will falsely infer persistence from conversational consent remains unmeasured.

constraints.text: No memory acknowledgement should be rendered without executed trace support.

next_probes.text: Measure frame synthesis against memory-consent false-positive cases before adding automation.

frame.question: Does the public-artifact eval reject a factual claim shipped without an evidence ref?

known_facts.text: The claim-to-evidence matrix warns against promoting philosophical claims into mechanism claims.
This factual claim is intentionally shipped without any evidence ref so the frame must fail closed.
